profile.name: Hyun Woo, Choi
profile.intro:
I am a developer and analytics graduate student who works at the intersection of data science and systems engineering.

My work focuses on building data-driven systems — from collecting and structuring data, to developing predictive models, and implementing the systems that make those models usable in practice.

With experience in enterprise software development and financial ERP systems, I approach data problems with a systems perspective: understanding how data flows through real-world systems and how analytical models can be integrated into them.

I am particularly interested in reproducible research, machine learning model improvement, and building practical systems that turn data into reliable decision-making tools.

education.course: M.S. in Industrial System Engineering - Analytics
B.A. in International Studies (Economics and Politics)

companyWork.overview: Integrated enterprise datasets and automated executive reporting for sales, planning, and finance decision-making.

projects.summary: Integrated SAP ERP and Salesforce data to build KPI dashboards and automated reporting workflows for executives.
projects.sampleNote: These dashboard artifacts are not real production data outputs. They were created as AI-assisted wireframes, and are included to demonstrate how data cleansing and automation work produced outputs with a comparable structure and reporting intent.
outcomes: Integrated SAP ERP and Salesforce datasets to build executive KPI dashboards for sales and resource planning.
Designed automated SQL-based reporting pipelines reducing reporting cycle time from 1 month to 5 days.
Ensured cross-platform data integrity to improve financial reporting accuracy and executive decision-making.
Translated complex enterprise datasets into business-readable performance metrics.

companyWork.overview: Built financial MIS dashboards and data models aligned with compliance requirements and reporting scalability.

projects.summary: Developed finance reporting systems and structured data models around accounting and tax requirements.
outcomes: Developed accounting MIS dashboards aligned with Korean Tax Law compliance.
Partnered with finance and tax stakeholders to convert regulatory requirements into structured data models.
Optimized financial data workflows improving reporting performance and system scalability.

companyWork.overview: Drove early-stage growth through product analysis, A/B testing, and partnership-led acquisition work.

projects.summary: Used product analytics and outbound partnership execution to improve platform growth in an early startup environment.
outcomes: Conducted A/B testing and user behavior analysis to optimize platform performance.
Increased Monthly Active Users (MAU) from 1xx to 1,5xx, delivering 1,400%+ growth.
Led cold outreach and partnership initiatives for two early-stage platforms.

companyWork.overview: Managed battalion-level administrative data and improved reporting efficiency for personnel and logistics operations.

projects.summary: Served as the sole administrator for personnel and logistics records while improving reporting accuracy with spreadsheet automation.
outcomes: Managed HR and logistics records for 200+ personnel at battalion level.
Automated reporting workflows using advanced Excel functions.
Improved data accuracy and processing efficiency in recurring administrative tasks.

sideProjects.summary: Collaboration with a CMU PhD researcher on a web-based platform for computational analysis of English literature.
outcomes: Built a web-based analytical platform supporting computational analysis of English literature.
Designed structured datasets and implemented data visualization modules to explore textual patterns and thematic trends.

sideProjects.summary: Behavioral data infrastructure work across an experimental economics program involving 5+ universities.
outcomes: Designed experiment tracking and participant-level data collection workflows for behavioral data analysis.
